$comment: Per-config request-validation settings for camunda-hub. enumCaseInsensitive: hub does not (yet) declare a position on enum case-insensitivity; default `false` emits case-only mutations as 400-expecting tests (conservative). authAbsentMode: 'all-secured' — the Hub Public API v2 is uniformly Bearer-authenticated via a single global `security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]` requirement and declares no `x-enforcement: conditional` schemes, so the 401 auth-absent surface is every secured op. authDenyMode: 'all-secured' — Hub checks body-validation (400) and resource-existence (404) before the authority gate (@PreAuthorize, 403), so only keyless, no-required-body, no-required-non-path-param secured ops are targeted (camunda/camunda-hub#25264). resourceFixtures (#352): map each resource-key name (path param OR body field) to the env var holding a REAL key the runner creates before tests run; wherever the generator would emit the filler 'x' for one of these, it emits `process.env[<ENV>] ?? 'x'` so a malformed-field test rides on a valid envelope (the path resource exists / the referenced project|folder is accessible) and reaches body validation (400) instead of a 404 lookup or 403 access check on 'x'. parentFolderKey reuses the folder fixture.
